A value-add repositioning opportunity adjacent to Mexico City's Medical South Zone — home to Médica Sur, INER, and the Instituto Nacional de Cardiología. Three distinct investment strategies are analyzed. See the Property & Comps tab for location details and market comparable evidence.

Capital Structure
ComponentAmount (MXN)Notes
Purchase Price$10,000,000Agreed acquisition cost
Equity — Down Payment$2,000,00020% LTV equity contribution
Seller Financing$8,000,00080% seller-carried note at 7% APR
Grace Period (Mo. 1–6)$46,667 / mo.Interest-only; full renovation window
Amortization (Mo. 7–66)$158,410 / mo.60-month principal + interest schedule
Total Financing Term66 Months6-mo grace + 60-mo amortization; note fully retired Mo. 66
Investment Strategies at a Glance
Scenario A

Fix & Flip — Exit Strategy

Renovate during the 6-month grace period and exit at ARV. Net profit is tax-free. Highest-conviction near-term strategy under current financing.

~28–35% IRR  Tax-Free Profit  Risk: Moderate
Scenario B

Medical Co-Living — Hold Strategy

6 furnished executive suites for medical residents. Requires debt restructuring to 15–20 year term. ROI turns strongly positive from Year 6 onward.

849% ROI at Yr 20  Requires Refi
Scenario C

Medical Consulting Offices

4 private medical suites for independent specialists. Highest revenue potential; requires land-use permit. ROI reaches 870% at Year 20.

870% ROI at Yr 20  Permit Required

Primary Recommendation: Scenario A — Fix & Flip

Under the current financing structure, Scenario A is the recommended strategy. The $158,410/month debt service from Month 7 creates an unsustainable cash deficit for hold strategies without prior refinancing. Scenarios B and C become viable only post-debt-restructuring into a 15–20 year institutional mortgage.

Scenario A — Fix & Flip

Near-term exit strategy using the 6-month grace period as a renovation window, then selling at After-Repair Value. Net profit is tax-free. Investor's own capital is costed at 5% APR.

✔ Net profit on this exit is treated as tax-free
★
Recommended Primary Strategy. Scenario A converts the below-market acquisition price into realized, tax-free capital gain through a full gut renovation during the interest-only grace period. Cost of the investor's capital is modeled at 5% APR opportunity cost.

Cost of Money Analysis — 5% APR Opportunity Cost
i
The investor's own capital is assigned an opportunity cost of 5% APR — the return that same capital could earn elsewhere (e.g., CETES or fixed income). This is layered on top of the net profit to compute a true risk-adjusted return. Even after this cost, Scenario A generates a compelling positive return in 6–8 months.
MonthEventCumul. Equity DeployedCumul. CoM @ 5% APRNote BalanceNet Profit (pre-CoM)Net Profit (post-CoM)ROI (pre-CoM)ROI (post-CoM)
0 Down payment & closing $2,000,000 $8,333 $8,000,000 — — — —
1 Reno tranche #1 + Interest-only $2,480,000 $18,667 $8,000,000 — — — —
2 Reno tranche #2 + Interest-only $2,960,000 $31,000 $8,000,000 — — — —
3 Reno tranche #3 + Interest-only $3,440,000 $45,333 $8,000,000 — — — —
4 Reno tranche #4 + Interest-only $3,920,000 $61,667 $8,000,000 — — — —
5 Reno tranche #5 + Interest-only $4,400,000 $80,000 $8,000,000 — — — —
6 Reno tranche #6 + Interest-only $4,880,000 $100,333 $8,000,000 $1.370M $1.270M 28.1% (56% ann.) 25.5%
7 Amortization payment #1 (if held) $5,038,410 $121,327 $7,888,257 $1.323M $1.202M 27.1% (46% ann.) 24.0%
8 Amortization payment #2 (if held) $5,196,819 $142,980 $7,775,862 $1.277M $1.134M 26.2% (39% ann.) 22.6%

CoM = opportunity cost of capital at 5% APR. Net profit is tax-free. Transaction costs of $750,000 MXN included. Green rows indicate viable exit windows. ROI (ann.) = annualized return for the hold duration.

Execution Recommendations
  • Lock down a reputable general contractor (GC) before closing with a fixed-price contract and penalty clauses — renovation overruns are the primary execution risk.
  • Pre-market the asset at Month 4–5 (pre-completion) to qualified medical/professional buyers via Propiedades.com, Lamudi, and a licensed broker network.
  • Retain a licensed valuador at Month 0 to establish baseline and confirm ARV before deploying renovation capital.
  • Structure the seller note with a no-penalty early-repayment clause to preserve exit flexibility at Month 6.
  • Budget a 10–15% contingency ($260K–$390K MXN) to absorb hidden defects. This is partially included in the $750K transaction cost estimate above.
⚠
Primary Risk: Renovation overruns or timeline delays beyond Month 6 will trigger full debt service of $158,410/month. Each month of delay costs approximately $21,000 in additional cost-of-money (5% APR on ~$4.9M), further compressing net profit. See the seller note amortization schedule on the Scenario B and C tabs.
For discussion purposes only. Not an offer to buy or sell securities. All projections are estimates subject to independent verification. Tax-free treatment assumes application of the primary residence or specific legal structure exemption under Mexican ISR law — confirm with a certified contador público before closing.
Scenario B — Medical Co-Living

Hold strategy repositioning as a 6-suite premium co-living facility for medical residents and fellows. Includes full seller note amortization schedule and year-by-year ROI.

⚠
Conditional Strategy. Financially viable only after debt restructuring into a 15–20 year institutional mortgage. Under current seller financing ($158,410/month), this scenario generates a ~$97K–$105K/month cash deficit. However, once the note is retired at Month 66, 100% of NOI flows debt-free and long-term ROI is exceptional.
Financial KPIs
Suites
6
Executive furnished
Rent / Suite / Month
$13K – $15K
MXN · All-inclusive
Gross Monthly Revenue
$78K – $90K
At full occupancy
Occupancy
85%
Stabilized assumption
NOI / Month
$53K – $61.2K
Pre-debt service
Cash Flow (Now)
–$97K to –$105K
MXN/month DEFICIT
ROI at Year 10
~211%
On $4M upfront capital
ROI at Year 20
~849%
On $4M upfront capital
✕
Cash Flow Alert — Current Terms: The $158,410/month debt service creates a $97K–$105K/month deficit. Over the 66-month note term, cumulative deficit before NOI offset reaches ~$5.2–$5.7M. Debt restructuring to a 20-year bank mortgage (est. $73K–$82K/month) is required for operational viability.
Viability Conditions
  • Debt Restructuring (Required): Convert to a 15–20 year bank mortgage at 8–10% APR. On $9M at 9% over 20 years: ~$80K/month — bringing the property to near break-even.
  • Purchase Price Reduction: Negotiate financed balance to $9M through LOI or inspection contingencies.
  • Operational Excellence: Partner with Médica Sur HR or residency coordinators for direct tenant pipelines; target 85%+ occupancy.
  • Renovation for Co-Living: Private bathrooms, study areas, smart locks, shared kitchen/laundry. All-in renovation: $1,800,000–$2,200,000 MXN.
Seller Note — Full Amortization Schedule (Months 1–66)

Phase 1 — Months 1–6

Grace Period (Interest Only)

$46,667/mo. No principal reduction. Renovation window. Note balance remains at $8,000,000.

Phase 2 — Months 7–60

Active Amortization

$158,410/mo. full P+I payments. Balance reduces from $8M to ~$931K by end of month 60.

Phase 3 — Months 61–66

Final Paydown

$158,410/mo. Note fully retired at Month 66. Balance reaches $0. All future NOI is debt-free.

For discussion purposes only. Not an offer to buy or sell securities.
Scenario C — Medical Consulting Offices

Commercial conversion into 4 private medical consulting offices (consultorios médicos). Highest revenue potential; requires regulatory approvals and debt restructuring.

⚠
Conditional — Regulatory Precondition. Requires a Commercial Land-Use Permit (Licencia de Uso de Suelo Comercial) from Alcaldía Tlalpan AND COFEPRIS sanitary certification. Do not close without written confirmation of zoning compliance. Debt restructuring is also required.
Financial KPIs
Consulting Offices
4
Private medical suites
Rent / Office / Month
$25K – $30K
MXN · Commercial medical
Gross Monthly Revenue
$100K – $120K
At full occupancy
Occupancy
80%
Commercial lease-up risk
NOI / Month
$65.6K – $78.7K
Pre-debt service
Cash Flow (Now)
–$79K to –$92K
MXN/month DEFICIT
ROI at Year 10
~222%
On $4.4M upfront capital
ROI at Year 20
~870%
On $4.4M upfront capital
✕
Cash Flow Alert: $158,410/month debt service creates a monthly deficit of $79,630–$92,750 MXN. Over 66 months, cumulative shortfall before NOI offset: ~$4.3–$5.0M. Debt restructuring is mandatory before this strategy can operate sustainably.
Regulatory Requirements
  • Land-Use Permit (Critical — Pre-Closing): Obtain a Certificado de Uso de Suelo from the Alcaldía Tlalpan before closing. Confirm zoning under PGDUE or applicable PDDU.
  • Rezoning Risk: If rezoning is required, timeline is 60–180 days and outcome is not guaranteed. Do NOT close without confirmation.
  • COFEPRIS Compliance: Each office requires clinical-grade ventilation, medical gas lines, and biosafety waste infrastructure. Budget $200,000–$400,000 MXN per office.
  • Commercial Leases: Minimum 2-year terms with INPC-indexed escalation clauses.

Long-Term Hold Model — Scenarios B & C

15–20 year wealth projection. Seller note fully retired at Month 66 (end Year 5+). From Year 6 onward, 100% of NOI flows debt-free. Rents escalate 3.5%/yr (INPC); property appreciates 6%/yr. ROI = net wealth / upfront capital − 1.

Phase 1 — Months 1–6

Zero-cost grace

No debt service. Full renovation window. Capital deployed; zero carrying cost.

Phase 2 — Years 1–5

Note paydown (deficit)

$158,410/mo debt service. NOI offsets partially. Cash deficit each year.

Phase 3 — Year 6 onward

Debt-free compounding

Note retired. Zero debt. 100% of NOI is yours. Property compounds at 6%/yr.

20 yrs

Annual Cash Flow

Cumulative Wealth Position

Property value Net wealth (property + cumul. cash) Cumulative cash position
Assumptions: Rent escalation 3.5%/yr (INPC-indexed) · Property appreciation 6%/yr · Seller note $8M at 7% APR, 60-month amortization from Month 7 through Month 66 · Phase 3 begins Year 6. ROI = Net Wealth / upfront capital − 1. All figures MXN. For discussion purposes only.
Market Analysis & SWOT

Submarket overview, validated ARV comparables, demand drivers, and strategic risk assessment.

Submarket Overview — Bosques de Tetlameya, Tlalpan

Bosques de Tetlameya is a mature, low-density residential colonia in the southern delegation of Tlalpan, Mexico City. The neighborhood features tree-lined streets, predominantly single-family and mixed-use housing stock from the 1960s–1990s, and a stable upper-middle-class residential profile. Its proximity to Periférico Sur and Insurgentes Sur provides strong vehicular connectivity to Coyoacán, Pedregal, and the Santa Fe corporate corridor.

The submarket's most critical attribute is its adjacency to the Zona Médica del Sur — one of Mexico City's most important tertiary healthcare clusters. This creates a persistent, institutionally-supported demand engine for both residential and clinical real estate that insulates the submarket from cyclical softening in the broader CDMX market.

Demand Drivers — Medical South Zone
InstitutionTypeRelevance to Investment
Médica SurPrivate tertiary hospital~3,000 employees; high-income specialist staff; demand for executive housing and consulting offices
INER — Inst. Nal. de Enf. RespiratoriasFederal research hospitalRotating residents, fellows, and visiting researchers; strong demand for furnished co-living
Instituto Nacional de CardiologíaFederal specialty hospitalCardiology subspecialty training center; 24-month rotation cycles create persistent housing demand
ISSSTE Tlalpan / HGZ ClinicsSocial security hospitalsAdditional healthcare workforce in catchment; secondary demand driver
SWOT Matrix
Strengths
  • Premium micro-location in the Medical South Zone — CDMX's highest-barrier-to-entry submarket
  • Seller financing at 7% APR vs. 10–13% typical for investment properties
  • 6-month interest-only grace period aligned with renovation execution window
  • 256 m² supports multiple unit configurations without major structural work
  • Strong institutional demand from Médica Sur, INER, and Instituto Nacional de Cardiología
Weaknesses
  • 66-month note term (including 6 grace + 60 amortizing) with $158K/month payment
  • Debt service exceeds realistic income under Scenarios B and C until note is retired
  • Renovation execution risk on a tight 6-month timeline
  • ISAI (~2%) and ISABI transfer taxes increase total acquisition cost
  • Specialized buyer pool — limited liquidity for medical mixed-use
Opportunities
  • High appreciation trajectory (6%/yr modeled) in supply-constrained submarket
  • Co-living and medical offices command 15–25% premium over standard residential
  • Bank refinancing at Year 1–2 (post-stabilization) could significantly improve hold yields
  • Growing medical resident population creates durable demand for executive housing
  • Future condominium regime conversion to unlock individual unit exit values
Threats
  • Banxico rate environment could increase refinancing cost for Scenarios B/C
  • Tlalpan land-use regulations may restrict commercial medical use without rezoning
  • Renovation cost inflation (materials, labor) in CDMX could compress Scenario A margins
  • Macroeconomic softening could delay ARV realization beyond Month 8
  • COFEPRIS regulatory changes could increase Scenario C buildout costs
  • Seller encumbrance risk on the financing note
Comparable data must be independently verified. Not an offer to buy or sell securities.
Suggested Next Steps — Action Plan

Sequential pre-closing and post-closing actions to de-risk the acquisition and maximize value across all three scenarios.

1
Immediate — Pre-LOI · Week 1–2
Structural & Physical Due Diligence
Commission a licensed perito (structural engineer/inspector) for a full physical inspection. Assess foundation integrity, electrical/plumbing systems, roof condition, and hidden defect exposure. Use findings to validate or revise the $2,600,000 renovation budget.
2
Week 1–2
Zoning Verification — Alcaldía Tlalpan
Visit the Alcaldía Tlalpan's Ventanilla Única to obtain a Certificado de Uso de Suelo. Confirm current zoning (habitacional mixto vs. commercial) and any restrictions. Prerequisite for Scenarios B and C.
3
Week 2–3
Submit Letter of Intent (LOI)
Draft LOI conditioned on: (a) satisfactory physical inspection; (b) clean title via Registro Público de la Propiedad; (c) property free of gravámenes; (d) seller acceptance of 6-month interest-only grace. Engage Notario Público for simultaneous title search.
4
Week 2–4
Renovation Bidding — Minimum 3 GCs
Issue SOW to at least 3 licensed general contractors. Require fixed-price bids with itemized breakdowns, timeline commitments, and penalty clauses. Select GC before closing to enable Day 1 mobilization when the grace period starts.
5
Week 3–4
Legal & Tax Counsel
Retain a Notario Público and a fiscal/tax advisor specialized in real estate. Key areas: ISAI calculation, ISR optimization on Scenario A profit (tax-free treatment), and structuring the seller financing note in a legally enforceable escritura.
6
Ongoing
Market Monitoring & Pre-Marketing
Track listings on Propiedades.com, Lamudi, and Inmuebles24 throughout renovation. Engage a broker at Month 4 for soft marketing. Pre-marketing at Months 4–5 is key to achieving a Month 6–8 exit.
i
Note on Scenarios B & C: If pursuing a hold strategy, engage a bank (Banorte, BBVA, Santander MX, Scotiabank MX) for pre-approval on a 15–20 year investment property mortgage at Months 6–12, once renovation is complete and rental income begins. Do not wait until Month 66 (note maturity) to begin refinancing discussions.
For discussion purposes only. All timelines are estimates. Legal counsel, a Notario Público, and a licensed tax advisor should be engaged before any binding commitment.
